plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the core conceptual pillars that define the Module 1 innovator mindset inside a hospital system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From a fragmented view of healthcare improvement to an integrated innovator mindset through a synthesized map of Module 1's core pillars
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly enumerates the module's ten core pillars, making this the central instructional payload. Because the value is in seeing the integrated whole rather than decomposing each pillar into separate cluster beats, this is kept dense as a singleton synthesis unit.
plan_units.title: Module 1 Core Mindset Pillars

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain why healthcare innovators must distinguish symptoms from root causes before choosing or building solutions.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first instinct to root-cause discipline through applying clinical diagnostic reasoning to system problems
plan_units.rationale: This is a dense, high-pedagogical-weight concept cluster because the source links several explanatory beats: diagnosis before prescription, solution fixation risk, symptom vs. root cause, and lead domino logic. Progressive disclosure helps move from familiar clinical reasoning to operational diagnosis.

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize the risk of falling in love with a solution instead of understanding the actual operational problem.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first instinct to root-cause discipline through applying clinical diagnostic reasoning to system problems
plan_units.rationale: This interstitial serves the tension beat by surfacing the common failure mode named in the source. It sharpens the contrast between visible pain and misdirected intervention.

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate visible symptoms from underlying system failure and infer why the true root cause matters most.

plan_units.rationale: This develop beat deepens the concept by connecting symptom/root-cause separation to the lead-domino effect, which is a central operational insight in the source.

plan_units.learning_objective: Distinguish healthcare innovation from routine clinical environments and identify how opportunity scanning supports intrapreneurial action.

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: This lesson plan distills Module 1 into four instructional moves: orient learners to the module's full mindset architecture, teach disciplined root-cause diagnosis through a clustered problem-first sequence, reframe hospital innovation as wicked and opportunity-rich, and close by linking mindset to the leadership identity work of Module 2. Clustering is used selectively for the highest-density concepts, while synthesis, prerequisite culture, and the final bridge remain intentionally keep-dense.

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: This lesson-plan slice covers the source-provided closing summary and bridge from Module 1 to Module 2. It consolidates the learner's completion of the innovator mindset, then transitions to the next module's emphasis on leadership identity, communication, and political savvy within complex health systems. The plan preserves the summary as a keep-dense synthesis moment, clusters the mindset-to-identity bridge into establish/tension/resolve beats, and explicitly captures the source-gap constraint so downstream production stays faithful to available materials.

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that Module 1 has established the learner's innovator mindset and prepared them to move from system reaction toward system redesign.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From completion acknowledgment to readiness for the next stage through a concise synthesis of what Module 1 has changed in the learner's posture.
plan_units.rationale: The source explicitly defines this as a summary slide and bridge, with a completion message and mindset framing. It is a synthesis/resolve moment whose value is the simultaneous whole, so it should remain a singleton keep-dense unit rather than be decomposed.
source_refs: Module 1 Complete: The Mindset is Set.
you understand the mindset of an innovator
You are no longer just reacting to the system;
you are preparing to redesign it.
plan_units.title: Module 1 Completion and Mindset Consolidation

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain that understanding innovation mindset is insufficient without cultivating the leadership identity, communication, and political skill needed to advance ideas inside a health system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in innovation mindset to recognition of leadership development needs through a bridge from ideas to the identity required to champion them.
plan_units.rationale: The bridge to Module 2 is explicitly described in the narration and visual storyboard. It introduces a distinct explanatory beat: the transition from having ideas to becoming the kind of leader who can champion them. This pedagogical transition warrants its own clustered treatment with progressive disclosure.
source_refs: Now that you understand the mindset of an innovator,
how do you begin your own personal journey?
In Module 2, we will move from Mindset to Identity.
plan_units.title: Bridge from Mindset to Leadership Identity

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify that innovation success in a matrixed health system depends far more on political savvy, communication skills, and leadership identity than on the idea alone.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From confidence in innovation mindset to recognition of leadership development needs through a bridge from ideas to the identity required to champion them.
plan_units.rationale: This is a core complicating beat within the bridge cluster. The source sharply contrasts idea quality with execution through leadership, making it a high-value tension point that deepens learner understanding of the transition into Module 2.

narration_script.duration_rationale: Moderate synthesis with a clear comparison; visuals are simple enough to support a concise, energetic close.
narration_script.narration_text: Congratulations—Module 1 is complete. On one side is system reaction: spending your days responding to whatever the system hands you. On the other side is system redesign: shaping what the system becomes. Compared with a reactive posture, the innovator mindset you’ve built positions you to diagnose problems before you prescribe fixes and to focus on root causes rather than symptoms. By contrast with chasing pain points, you’re now ready to redesign how care is delivered—inside the politics, budgets, and legacy systems you already live in.

narration_script.duration_rationale: Bridge beat needs two-part seam and brief framing; definitions are short and supported by the cards.
narration_script.narration_text: Quick stitch from where you’ve just landed: you’ve built the innovator mindset and begun moving from reaction toward redesign. The main message now is personal—ideas don’t advance themselves in a matrixed system; who you are as a leader determines whether they travel. Mindset is the foundation, leadership identity is the bridge that carries your vision, and political skill is the engine that moves it through relationships and power. This is the crossing we make next.

visual_references.narration_cue: mindset is insufficient without leadership identity and political skill

narration_script.duration_rationale: Tension beat that names three capabilities; keep crisp while the cards carry structure.
narration_script.narration_text: Here’s the rub: the idea isn’t the bottleneck. Success rests far more on political savvy—reading the room, mapping stakeholders, navigating power—and on communication that turns complex concepts into a narrative people want to act on. It also rests on a steady leadership identity others can trust. That’s why the same idea thrives under one champion and stalls under another; the difference is the leader’s ability to move the system.

your idea is only as powerful as your ability to lead others toward it

As we enter Module 2, we’ll shape your identity, sharpen your message, and practice coalition‑building. See you in Module 2.

desmond_handoff.handoff_text:
## Sanctum handoff: Descript assembly for APP finish (5-segment lesson)

Overview
- Project context: Descript assembly handoff for an APP-produced narrated lesson. Final path should preserve segment order, narration integrity, caption alignment, and the intended pacing per the manifest.
- Bundle inputs: assembly-bundle/, including segment-manifest.yaml, audio/, captions/, visuals/ and motion/ as present.
- Segment count: 5 (seg-01 through seg-05) with narration durations pulled from the envelope payload:
  - seg-01: 36.415 s
  - seg-02: 29.362 s
  - seg-03: 28.343 s
  - seg-04: 23.510 s
  - seg-05: 27.559 s
- Visual policy: motion clips are playback; stills are reference/poster; no new creative is allowed beyond finishing.
- Caption policy: import VTTs and align to VO; adjust as needed to stay in sync with narration.
- Naming conventions: follow Descript terms (sequence/scene/track) as per team memory (MEMORY.md) and governance (BOND.md).
- Governance: if a requested change would alter pedagogy or wording, pause and route to production governance (document the gap).

Preflight checklist (editor runs before export)
- Verify project frame rate and resolution match media and manifest expectations.
- Confirm the narration is locked to the script; avoid altering wording without governance.
- Import and verify all assets: audio seg-01…seg-05.mp3; captions seg-01.vtt…seg-05.vtt; any visuals/motion assets present.
- Check loudness policy per project (mention from MEMORY/BOND); apply ducking if VO overlaps with any music or ambient audio.
- Chapter markers (if used in the manifest) added and synced; ensure scene transitions reflect segment boundaries.
- CILEAN: ensure all captions are within reasonable drift tolerance of audio (typical target: < 200 ms alignment drift; adjust as needed).
- Timeline integrity: ensure each segment’s duration on the timeline matches the measured duration; if mismatch exists, prefer keeping VO pacing and adjusting caption timing to maintain sync.
- No new creative: if a change is requested that affects pedagogy, flag for governance; document the gap.
- Export plan: decide between burn-in captions vs sidecar; render master; prepare LMS-ready package if required.

Segment-by-segment plan (import targets, sync intent, and finishing notes)

Segment 01 (seg-01)
- Import targets:
  - Audio: assembly-bundle/audio/seg-01.mp3
  - Captions: assembly-bundle/captions/seg-01.vtt
  - Visuals/Motion: if present (assembly-bundle/visuals/seg-01/ or motion/seg-01.*)
- Timeline intent:
  - VO track: seg-01 narration placed at sequence start
  - Visuals: use corresponding still/video as per manifest; maintain sync with VO
  - Captions: align to VO timing; ensure first caption starts with the VO first syllable
- Finishing notes:
  - If narration duration exceeds visual duration, hold key visual frame or loop the motion as policy allows; clearly document any hold policy used.
  - Confirm exact alignment of first and last caption cues to VO.

Segment 02 (seg-02)
- Import targets:
  - Audio: assembly-bundle/audio/seg-02.mp3
  - Captions: assembly-bundle/captions/seg-02.vtt
  - Visuals/Motion: as available
- Timeline intent:
  - Maintain same track structure as seg-01; keep consistent pacing and pacing cues
- Finishing notes:
  - Check for caption drift; correct in Descript so cue times remain in sync with audio.

Segment 03 (seg-03)
- Import targets:
  - Audio: assembly-bundle/audio/seg-03.mp3
  - Captions: assembly-bundle/captions/seg-03.vtt
  - Visuals/Motion: as available
- Timeline intent:
  - Ensure transition from seg-02 to seg-03 is smooth; preserve narrative continuity
- Finishing notes:
  - If the slide/visual reference differs from VO length, apply hold or appropriate motion reference per policy.

Segment 04 (seg-04)
- Import targets:
  - Audio: assembly-bundle/audio/seg-04.mp3
  - Captions: assembly-bundle/captions/seg-04.vtt
  - Visuals/Motion: as available
- Timeline intent:
  - Align VO and captions; maintain consistent lighting/motion token if present
- Finishing notes:
  - Address any duration mismatch between VO and visual assets with policy-based holds.

Segment 05 (seg-05)
- Import targets:
  - Audio: assembly-bundle/audio/seg-05.mp3
  - Captions: assembly-bundle/captions/seg-05.vtt
  - Visuals/Motion: as available
- Timeline intent:
  - Final segment; ensure smooth end-of-lesson transition; confirm end cue timing
- Finishing notes:
  - If this segment ends with a visual hold, ensure the hold preserves the final VO cadence.

Motion vs still policy and explicit references
- Movement assets (motion) should be applied on a dedicated Visuals/Motion track; stills should be used as reference/poster when no motion is required.
- If a segment requires a different finish (hold, loop, cover frame), specify the exact policy and implement accordingly; do not improvise timing or pacing that alters instructional intent.

Static segments and pacing
- Static (still) segments should hold for the narration duration unless the guide explicitly calls for a different pacing instruction.
- If a specific segment requires a shorter/longer visual reference than narration, document the difference and apply hold/loop/cover according to policy.

Quality checks and export plan
- Captions: verify speech-to-text accuracy, line breaks, and punctuation; ensure closed captions align with narration and language is appropriate for audience.
- Audio: verify VO loudness is consistent with project policy; apply any automatic ducking if needed.
- Visuals: verify alignment with audio and captions; ensure no black frames or timing issues.
- Chapter markers: if used, ensure correct placement and labeling.
- Export: render master MP4 with burn-in captions or sidecar captions as per project spec; also export a separate captions file if required by LMS or hosting.
- Handoff notes: document any policy deviations or gaps observed during assembly and route for governance if needed.

Appendix: session artifacts and governance
- Append this session’s notes to memory (MEMORY.md) with timestamp and touched conventions.
- If any guidance in this handoff conflicts with the owner’s stated conventions, escalate and annotate the gap rather than overwriting policy.
- Save a session log under sessions/YYYY-MM-DD.md with the rationale for decisions and any follow-ups.

Notes
- API surfaces are evolving; if you hit an automation wall, reference the automation advisory and the current Descript API cache/docs.
- If the manifest or assets differ from expected, flag and attach exact diffs to governance.
